publishedAbstract: * S3 Lifecycle Policy configured in NooBaa / Multicloud Object Gateway (MCG) to delete objects after a specific number of days does not result in a reduction of storage space. * Objects appear to persist despite the policy being active and reported as "successful" in the UI. * This is frequently observed with buckets used by Loki where massive amounts of log data are stored. * Background workers like object_reclaimer are active, but storage usage remains high.
publishedTitle: S3 Lifecycle Policy fails to remove Loki objects in NooBaa due to bucket versioning
